name: PULMONARY HEMORRHAGE
dominant_finding: cluster of avidly enhancing "nodular" bronchial arteries in posterior mediastinum below
distribution: cluster of avidly enhancing "nodular" bronchial arteries in posterior mediastinum below
discriminating_features: cluster of avidly enhancing "nodular" bronchial arteries in posterior mediastinum below
abnormal bronchial arteries show tortuosity, dilatation, hypervascularity, neovascularity,
bilateral patchy / confluent ground-glass opacities / consolidation
may delineate underlying etiology (bronchiectasis, lung cancer, TB, pulmonary embolism)

HRCT: bilateral patchy / confluent ground-glass opacities / consolidation
may delineate underlying etiology (bronchiectasis, lung cancer, TB, pulmonary embolism)
NUC: typically matched defect on V/Q scan in bronchial artery bleeding
bronchial blood flow may increase by 300% in the weeks following pulmonary
CT: cluster of avidly enhancing "nodular" bronchial arteries in posterior mediastinum below
abnormal bronchial arteries show tortuosity, dilatation, hypervascularity, neovascularity,
ANGIO: vasodilated enlarged tortuous bronchial arteries → bronchial-to-pulmonary-artery

multidetector CT mapping prior to angiography reduces the rate of catheterization
clinical.Prognosis: 50-100% mortality rate of conservatively treated massive hemoptysis; death
clinical.Rx: transcatheter particulate embolization of bronchial aa. using polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) +
